rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE annexes by ensuring all referenced symbols are visible or properly declared in the same annex.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type scopes.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid type errors.
Ensure assumptions and guarantees reference concrete bounds or named constants when defining input envelopes or output ranges.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE annexes are either package-level, property sets, or explicitly declared within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must declare helper functions or variables in the same scope where they are used.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly unless represented through valid variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers that are not visible or declared in the same AGREE annex.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
